TextENGNew: Oh, really. So your folks came back, Kei-chan!
They reeked of energy drinks, and their eyes were bloodshot. Sounds like they pulled two full nights without sleep and went through one hell of a crunch. Adults have it rough.
Keiichi-kun's father was a painter, right? Painters have it rough too, huh.
changeReason: Changed 'artist' to 'painter' or vice versa for 画家, a more specific term

TextENGNew: Now that you mention it, that's right. I'd love to see what kind of paintings he makes at least once!
...A painter, huh.
changeReason: Changed 'artist' to 'painter' or vice versa for 画家, a more specific term

TextENGNew: I've seen a few of the watercolors and acrylics he has on display, but I can't help thinking he does other work besides that.
...What kind of work does my old man actually do, anyway?
...My old man's atelier is strictly off-limits. Someday I want to sneak a look.
So in the end, they were only out of the house for two nights? Heh heh heh, that was a short conquest window!
So? Did you manage to clear the route for your girl of choice, Kei-chan?! Having your parents away is a dating-sim protagonist basic, after all!
changeReason: Changed 'eroge' (adult game) to 'dating sim' for ギャルゲー, a meaningful genre distinction

TextENGNew: ...You earned lots of affection points and properly raised the flag, indeed.
I have no idea what you all are talking about...
Then that means Keiichi-kun doesn't have to cook for himself anymore, huh. What did you make last night, I wonder? I wonder?
Mm... mmm.
...When it came down to it... I didn't cook for myself even once.
Last night, um... yeah, stir-fried vegetables.
Ohh! Looks like Kei-chan can make something that actually counts as cooking now.
Hmmm~☆ So you made stir-fried vegetables. Did it turn out tasty, I wonder? I wonder?!
...Rena-san, you're stifling. Why are you clinging all over me...?
...A delicious meal, and then washing the dishes together in harmony, it is.
H-hauuuuu~☆ I wish I had that too!!
Good grief! I told you you're stifling!!
...Huh? What are you talking about??
Mion, thoroughly led in circles, asked me about it, but all I could do was awkwardly look away and play dumb.
Oh, right! Hey, does everyone have plans for this afternoon? You don't, right? No way you do, right!!
Today is Saturday.
A wonderful day when the whole afternoon is free from school.
Incidentally, Saturdays at Hinamizawa's school are a little different from Saturdays at ordinary city schools.
A lot of students bring bentos, eat lunch, and then stay to play around the school building or the yard.
You could say the school itself becomes a playground for the kids...
There's a mountain out back, and if you head downhill there's a stream.
changeReason: Changed plural 'mountains' to singular 'mountain' matching JPN 山

TextENGNew: You can avoid the harsh sunlight and chat in the classroom,
or hide in the concrete pipes and play hide-and-seek.
Of course, they're free to just go home too.
I hear some kids go back to help water the fields or take over watching the shop.
And if Mion is the one bringing up an afternoon like that, then it has to be a huge club event!
Sadly, before I could join the club, I had a little work to do.
...Work? That's unlucky.
What kind of work could it be, I wonder. Helping your father with his painting job, perhaps?
Hmm... Maybe you'll be a model for your father's painting? ...A model?
Nude?
Hau~~~♪
...When I peered into Rena's eyes... I saw myself stark naked, a single rose in my mouth, striking a suspicious pose?!
No way, no way! That alone is absolutely never happening, not even for a million!
To scatter Rena's delusion, I grabbed her by the head and shook her around.
Hau~~~~!! Stoooop iiiit!!
...So, anyway. Putting the fun delusions aside, I've got a little work to do at noon. If it's fine for me to join up after that, then I'm in.
Work at noon? Hoh. That sounds kind of cool, doesn't it? What is it? A meal with VIPs from all walks of life? A business lunch?!
...Uh. ...As sad as it is to say, you're right.
Eeeehhh?!
Everyone yelps in utter disbelief...

That's right.
Today, the whole family has to work.
Because of Dad's work, we're hosting a home party with around ten people from the publishing side.
The day after pulling two all-nighters, he's inviting work contacts over for a party, so I can only stand in awe of that stamina.
...Being an adult is rough.
Of course, it's stifling beyond belief.
Putting on polite smiles for a bunch of middle-aged guys I don't even know is downright exhausting.
But... they're Dad's important business partners, so it'd be bad to put them in a foul mood...
So, for one reason and another, today gets very suffocating starting at noon.
Well, well! What a sharp-looking child...!

Keiichi, this is Honda-san from the publishing company.
And this is Inoue-san from the printing company.
Go on, greet them.

H-hello, nice to meet you.
I'm Keiichi, his son.
Thank you for always looking after my father...

Keiichi-kun, do you draw like your father?
I'd very much like to see your drawings too!
My old man works in that pretentious profession called painting.
changeReason: Repeats artist/painter distinction for 画家 in a new context

TextENGNew: Though... I don't really know what kind of works he paints.
I'm not especially interested, and I don't even want to see them.
Dad's stingy too, and hardly ever shows me anything.
I'm not a little kid anymore, so even if he had nude paintings, I wouldn't care at all.
If Keiichi-kun studies properly under his father, he'll surely become a wonderful artist like him.

...Um, just between us...
Is my old man... really that amazing a painter?
changeReason: Changed 'artist' to 'painter' or vice versa for 画家, a more specific term

TextENGNew: I make up my mind... and ask in a whisper.
It's kind of absurd for me, his own son, to be asking something like this, but...
Then the man gave this sleazy little grin and thumped both my shoulders hard.

Oh, absolutely!!
He really is... a truly wonderful artist!!
S-so... I see... That's good to hear...
There aren't any purchase limits,
they do fan service,
changeReason: Changed pronoun from singular 'he' to plural 'they', altering who is being described

TextENGNew: and they always reply to fan letters!
changeReason: Changed pronoun from singular 'he' to plural 'they', altering who is being described

TextENGNew: They properly grasp what the public wants, and they're passionate about their research!! Taking the socks off is absolutely a no-go!
The same goes for glasses and uniforms!
Maebara-sensei understands that perfectly!! Every event has massive lines at the wall...,
U-um, excuse me... Aren't paintings... things you hang on walls...??
``Not at all!@k@vS19/00/cusB50002. In the beginning, everyone starts in the islands!``
changeReason: New preserves convention-specific term 島中 as 'island-center' where old used generic 'in the islands'

TextENGNew: ``And then to their corners,``
changeReason: New translation preserves convention-specific Japanese terms (島中/島角 = convention booth layout terminology)

TextENGNew: ``and finally climbing up to the corners of the larger paths!``
changeReason: New translation preserves convention-specific Japanese terms (島中/島角 = convention booth layout terminology)

TextENGNew: And the summit you finally reach is the wall!!
You too! Please become a great artist who can exhibit in a joint booth with Maebara-sensei!! I'm cheering for you!!!
E-even if you look at me with those burning eyes full of expectation...
changeReason: Old fabricates 'fine to have passion for things you like' — JPN そんな熱い眼差しで期待されても means 'even if you look at me with those burning expectant eyes' — completely different meaning.

TextENGNew: I don't really get it... but anyway, it seems the world of artists is deep...
It's probably a muuuch deeper world than I think.@k...That's what I'll decide to believe.
Just then, I noticed my mom calling me from over there.
Keiichi. It's a phone call. From a friend.
Lucky!
If I use a call from a friend as an excuse, I might be able to slip out of this cramped business party.
I hurried up to the front hall and snatched the receiver that had been left on hold.
Hello! It's meee!
Hello, it's meee? That is no greeting at all, Keiichi-san. You should study your manners of speech a little more.
...Though I think the one saying that has the shakiest speech of all.
What, it's Satoko! It's rare for you to call me.
This is no time to be saying carefree things! More importantly, Keiichi-san, when are you going to get over here?!
S-sorry... Things are dragging on over here too... They just won't let me go... I want to join the club as soon as I can...
That's not it, Keiichi-san! Today isn't club activity. This is real combat!!
Satoko's voice is different from usual.
...How can I put it, there's a tense air about it!
R-real combat?! That doesn't sound peaceful at all! What's going on?!
I'll explain in detail once we meet! Anyway... this is an absolute crisis!! At this rate, we're going to lose!!
What is this, what the hell is going on?!
No way... a fight?!
I don't know who they are or where they're from, but messing with my Hinamizawa friends is going way too far!!
Yeah! Got it!! I'll be there to back you up right away!! Where are you?!
At the grounds of Okinomiya Elementary School! If you come to the station, there are signs, so you'll find it!! Just hurry, as fast as you can!!
The school grounds...!
A setup where a lot of people are about to crash into each other... Could this be a massive brawl?!
Who in the world is the enemy?! Uoooooh!! Now I'm fired up!!
Got it! I'm coming right now!! Should I bring a weapon too?! Maybe I'll bring a metal bat!!
You have a metal bat?! That's reassuring indeed!! We'll be waiting for you!!
The call cuts off with a hard click.
I've got to hurry!!
Oh, Keiichi. I've been looking for you. Deguchi-san here says, for the next dōjin magazine...
More importantly than that! Got a bat?! A metal bat! If not... anything long will do!!
...I-if anything long will do... Dad's got golf clubs in the shed.
It doesn't have the impact of a bat... but a golf club is a fine weapon too!
Since it's lighter than a bat, it might even be easier for me to handle!
Thanks! I'll borrow that!! I just got something I have to run and do at a friend's place!! Sorry!!
H-hey, Keiichi!? Wait...!!
Snatching up a golf club, I jumped onto my bike and tore straight toward town...!
Hang on, Satoko!
I'm coming right now!!
Hold out until I get there!!
Truth is, I didn't really know where Okinomiya Elementary was.
Satoko's call sounded so urgent that I bolted out without even asking for detailed directions...
...I think she said there'd be signs once I got to Okinomiya Station...
Just then, a bicycle bell rang behind me, calling out to me.
...Hello there.
Oh! Rika-chan!! ...Huff... huff!
...Keiichi, that was fast. You rushed as hard as you could.
Damn!
This isn't the time to be standing here out of breath! I have to hurry!!
S-sorry!! Where's Okinomiya Elementary?!
...I'll guide you. Follow me.
Rika-chan took off on her bicycle. I chased after her.
Satoko suddenly called me out... what in the world is going on?!
...At first, we had the upper hand.
...But then, partway through, they brought in reinforcements. That's cheating.
changeReason: Changed 'helper' to 'reinforcements' for 助っ人, shifting the scale/military tone

TextENGNew: Reinforcements?!
changeReason: Changed 'helper' to 'reinforcements' for 助っ人, shifting the scale/military tone

TextENGNew: The moment they saw they couldn't win, they called in backup? What a cowardly bunch!!
...One after another, everyone got struck down. ...We can't even put up a fight.
...Gulp. ...Sounds like a seriously formidable enemy...
Anyway, details can wait! We don't have time, right?! Hurry!
...I'm leaving it to you, nanodesu.
changeReason: New preserves speech tic 'nanodesu' (なのです) absent from old translation

TextENGNew: With Rika-chan leading the way, we head straight for the elementary school's grounds!
On the way, I asked Rika-chan...
Unbelievably, every single club member except me is already in this fight.
...Damn, so this is what Mion was calling everyone together for!
If I'd known that, I would've blown off the work at home!! ...Too late for regrets now.
Rika-chan says we had the upper hand at first, but the other side got fed up and sent in a powerful ringer.
...To counter that, the club members challenged them one after another... and still couldn't beat them!!
The whole club team can't win even all together?! What kind of monster are we talking about?!
...They're incredible, nanodesu. The enemy is orthodox.
changeReason: New preserves speech tic 'nanodesu' (なのです) absent from old translation

TextENGNew: ...Orthodox...
Not some delinquent street-brawling style, but a true orthodox fighter who's properly trained in karate and the like...?!
...Keiichi, you're our only hope now, nanodesu.
changeReason: New preserves speech tic 'nanodesu' (なのです) absent from old translation

TextENGNew: Okinomiya Elementary comes into view.
...A huge uproar of voices is spilling from the grounds!!
Uoooooooh!! Mion! Rena! Satokooo!! I'm coming right now!! I'll crush the whole lot of them!!
A mass of bicycles had been thrown down in front of the school gate.
I dump my own bike beside them the same way.
I can tell a huge number of people are surging onto the grounds.
...So it's a full-on riot with both sides all mixed together?!
I take up the golf club.
Whump! One sharp swing.
It clings tight to my palms.
All right!! The feel's not bad! I'm gonna rampage!!
Swinging it around, I storm through the school gate at full tilt...!!!
Follow me, Rika-chan!! I’ll show you the peerless exploits of one man, Maebara Keiichi!!!
...Waa. Keiichi is very dependable. He’s going to go on a rampage with a golf club.
Rika-chan praises my gallantry with a few dry little claps!
With that at my back, Maebara Keiichi the man now marches into the jaws of death!!
...But Keiichi. ...Why is it a golf club today?
What choice did I have?! I thought a bat would be better too, but we don’t have one at home. It’s way better than bare hands, so at least I brought a golf club!!
Very good, very good. I shall give Keiichi head pats. I’m looking forward to seeing you rampage with that golf club.
Even in an emergency, Rika-chan stretches up on tiptoe and asks for my head.
H-hey! Is this really the time to fool around?! ...Huh?
KRAAAANG!!
At that moment, an especially loud metallic crack rang out, followed by a huge cheer.
Left field! Aaah...! It got through!
Runnn!! You can make second, second!!
Center!! Stay calm!! Send it to third!!
Center fields it, waits a beat, then throws to third.
If he panicked and made a wild throw, it could turn into a triple.
changeReason: Old says a wild pitch 'might get there'; new correctly says a wild throw 'could turn into a triple,' which is the proper baseball outcome for 暴投.

TextENGNew:
They deliberately give up second and throw to third with room to spare... The bench’s instructions are impressively solid...!
...Wait... huh?
Ah, Keiichi-kun came! Satoko-chan, Satoko-chan, Keiichi-kun came for us!!
``You're late, late, late!!@k@vS19/04/500400510. Loafing around is only okay when you do it at the bread shop!!``
changeReason: Changed from localized idiom ('loafing at bread shop') to literal Japanese idiom ('selling oil at gas station')

TextENGNew: ``That boy, Maebara-kun... can he really serve as our pinch hitter...?@k@vS19/10/501000002. Our opponent is Kameda-kun, the left-handed ace of that powerhouse school, you know?@k@vS19/10/501000003. To think someone could crack a Koshien-class pitcher... just what kind of helper is he...?!``
changeReason: Old ends with 'He must be a god' (fabricated); new ends differently. Both convey amazement at the batter's skill but old adds content not in the Japanese.

TextENGNew: It’s okay. If it’s Keiichi-kun... I’m sure he’ll turn this game around! He’s truly dependable!
A ripple of shock!! ...What did you say?!
A pinch hitter who can take down Kameda the lefty... the one known as the Bullet Fastball?!
The undefeated demonic pitcher who kept racking up no-hitters in the regional qualifiers...
He can take down that super high school pitcher who’s already on pro scouts’ radar...!?
That pathetic little pitcher isn't even worth Keiichi-san's notice!! He'll send a ball like that flying clear over the back screen without breaking a sweat!!
A restless murmur ripples through the crowd!!
A slugger like that was in this prefecture?! W-we didn't know! He wasn't on our radar!! Hey! The scouting director over there just got to his feet...!! Hey! Get a picture!!
Full name: Maehara Keiichi... Search the database at once and send his record to headquarters!!!
changeReason: Changed name romanization from 'Maebara' to 'Maehara' for 前原

TextENGNew: Which one is Maehara Keiichi?! Get photos, photos!!
changeReason: Changed name romanization from 'Maebara' to 'Maehara' for 前原

TextENGNew: With Satoko in front, a huge pack of sports reporters raised their hulking cameras and searched for the rumored Maehara Keiichi.
changeReason: Changed name romanization from 'Maebara' to 'Maehara' for 前原

TextENGNew: So dumbfounded by what had happened... I stood there frozen, still holding my golf club raised...
Keiichi-san, Keiichi-san, over hereee...!!!
The cameramen stared at me through their viewfinders, and just like me... they too... turned to stone.
...In the hand of the dashing pinch-hitter Maehara Keiichi was... a golf... club...
changeReason: Changed name romanization from 'Maebara' to 'Maehara' for 前原

TextENGNew: No one seemed able to understand why I was holding it... and by now, not even I did...
Miiin, minminminmin... Sweat dripped off me in heavy drops...
The one who finally broke that unbearably heavy silence... was Rena.
...Um... Kei... Keiichi-kun.
Y-yeah...
May I... ask you one thing...?
...Please...
Don't ask.
...Along with the sweat pouring from my face, a desperate plea spilled out. Somehow, it seemed to reach both of them...
I slipped through the reporters, silent as a grave, parting them like Moses splitting the sea.
...But Moses probably didn't sneak through his parted sea this furtively...
I-if this was a baseball game, you should've said so from the start!!!
Eeeeh?! Didn't I say it?!
First of all, if I tell you to bring a bat and come to the field, what are you supposed to think of besides baseball?! I mean... how do you mess up badly enough to come up with a golf club?!
Mmm... I'm sure, I'm sure! Trying to brighten up the team when the mood got dark and get everyone fired up... I-I think Keiichi-kun did it on purpose. ...I think!
Rena gives me a strained smile and does her absolute best to cover for me.
Everyone, please believe that... P-please...
...Keiichi loves golf, you know. ...pat pat.
...Rika-chan keeps patting my head the whole time, thoroughly pleased with herself.
I brush that hand away, pinch the suspenders on Rika-chan's back, and lift her up like a kitten.
...Mii.
Riiika-chaaaan...!! Why didn't you tell me it was baseball when we met at the station earlier?! If you had, I wouldn't have made such a complete fool of myself!!
...Nipah~☆
Rika-chan shows me an innocent, sunshine-bright smile, as pure as can be.
Ugh... s-so bright...
That dazzling Rika-chan smile almost makes me want to forgive her for anything...
But!
If she thinks acting like that means I'll forgive her, then Rika-chan is underestimating me!!
And so, this is your punishment. ...Rena.
...Mii?
Still holding Rika-chan up, I present her to Rena.
...That's right.
At kittenized Rika-chan, Rena's excitement is already at absolute maximum!!
H-hau~~~~!!! Mine, mine!! Rena gets her~!! Hau~!! So cuuute~~~!!!
Boing!
Whirl-whirl!!
Rena shoots out her tongue like a chameleon, wraps Rika-chan up tight, and devours her!
Rika-chan is so cuuute! Rika-chan is so cuuute!! Hau~~!!
Mii! Miiii~~!!
...Still clamped tight like a vise, Rena luxuriates in a long, sticky cheek-rub.
Cheek against cheek, squeezed tight. Rika-chan's face looks as if it's being crushed against Rena's...
...I do not know what happened, but... it seems Rika has only herself to blame.
Yeah. I've only just realized lately that Rika-chan isn't just cute...
Hauu~~!! Keiichi-kun gave me permission~!! I'm taking her home~~~~!!!
Mii!!
Miii~~!!
For now, as punishment, we decide to leave her like that for about three minutes.
...By the way, Satoko. This emergency summons was about baseball?
Yes, it was. I heard you were having a family home party? I did do something rather rude. But in times like this, a comrade's crisis takes priority over family, of course!
...I think that's supposed to be my line.
Well, it was my old man's business party, anyway.
Honestly, I was happy they called me.
So what does this crisis situation look like? ...Six to seven. So our team is the one that's behind, huh.
...Huh? Bottom of the ninth, two outs?! What the hell?! This game's almost over already...!
If Keiichi-san had come earlier, we'd have had a little more room to work with! Do you have any idea how hard we struggled to drag it out this far?!
...Alright, alright, I'm sorry. So? What's my job? You want me to pinch-hit? Then leave it to me! I may not look it, but before I moved, I used to go to the batting cages a lot.
Whenever my old man went to blow off steam, he'd drag me along. If it's contact hitting, I'm fairly confident...,
ZUBBAAAAAAAAAAAAAAN!!
...An incredible sound I'd never heard before rang out.
...The catcher's mitt shuddered violently, a faint cloud of dust rose, and it told me that ball was what had made that monstrous sound.
...Hold on, hey... what in the world was that insane blazing fastball just now?
...Surprised? Yeah. That's a real Koshien pitcher. You don't know Kameda-kun, the left-handed high school ace who's famous even in the sports papers?
Sorry, I don't. ...Wait, Mion?! W-what's with that outfit?!
The Mion who showed up was... unbelievably, wrapped head to toe in bandages! W-what on earth happened?!
Um... since Keiichi-kun got here halfway through, we should explain from the beginning, right?
Oh, huh. Rika-chan, are you done already?
Yeah. I had lots of fun with all the cute stuff.
All fluffy and soft and squishy~☆
Hauu~~, those cheeks alone are enough for three bowls of rice~♪
......Mii. I can never get married now...
...Man, what kind of cheeks does Rika-chan have if they go with three bowls of rice?☆
...I somehow keep my reason and stop myself before fantasy mode can switch on. ...I'm an adult.
Then explain it to me. ...What kind of wrong turn leads to a Koshien pitcher coming in as relief for a sandlot showdown like this...?
Everyone looks at each other. Then Rena speaks for the group.
Yeah. At first, it was just a normal baseball match.
The Hinamizawa Fighters and the Okinomiya Titans have a modest little rivalry going on. So this time too, things were modestly getting super heated.
``Well, in simpler terms, it was an intramural match.@k@vS19/04/500400528. A match to settle the grudge between Hinamizawa and Okinomiya!``
changeReason: Changed 'intramural match' to 'school showdown' -- different type of competition

TextENGNew: Even if we call it a grudge match, it's really all friendly, though.
...So then why are our club members taking part? Some people are in uniforms, others are in regular clothes. ...And we're not actually a baseball club to begin with, right?
Ahahahahaha. In other words... yeah, it's pretty loose.
Today, they just happened to have openings on the team. So we figured why not, and ended up crashing in at the last minute.
I see.
If a team is short on players, then gender and grade don't matter, and people join as helpers.
That all-mixed-together feeling is exactly what our school is like.
From what Rika-chan told me, you were pushing them around hard at the start, right?
......Mii is an amazing batter. Home runs to right and left.
Mion proudly puffed out her chest.
Then, apparently, somewhere on her body started hurting, and she let out a pitiful groan...
Y-Yes! Every time Mion-san stepped up, she blasted a huge homer that cleared the bases!
...Unlike Satoko, she can actually put the bat on the ball.
Rikaaa!
...Well, I think I get the picture. Up until that Koshien pitcher showed up, it felt like an easy win.
That's right. Early on, the game was completely flowing our way. We kept getting on base and pressing them hard, even if we couldn't turn it into runs...
By pure chance, Kameda-kun came to watch today's game.
Apparently, the high school this guy Kameda attends is a famous regular at Koshien in our prefecture.
And Kameda, the ace of their baseball club... sounds like he's seriously good.
...Come to think of it, seeing several cameramen wearing press armbands at a backwoods sandlot game really is strange...
...It seems Kameda used to be on the Okinomiya Titans when he was little.

...You can see where this is going, right? His old team started falling behind, so he ended up jumping in as a relief pitcher... After that, it was one after another. Forget getting on base, we couldn't even hit a foul.
But still!! A Koshien pitcher barging in like that... that's cheating in a sandlot game!!!
The boys in Hinamizawa Fighters uniforms... drooped, utterly dejected...
Most of them were familiar faces.
Huh, they're all my classmates... Ah, right. Hinamizawa only has the one school.
Oh, Tomita-kun, Okamura-kun, isn't it? So you guys were on the baseball team too!
...M-Maebara-san............ uuu...
Their uniforms are brand-new, hardly even dirty yet, and that makes them look so fresh-faced.
...In the stands, I can see handmade banners with their names on them.
...I see. Their families came out to cheer for their debut game today.
...I hear Okamura's bat was a present from his father for today.
The brand-new bat stands out.
...If someone gave you something like that, you couldn't call yourself a man if you didn't shine out here.
They practiced and practiced for today, doing endless practice swings. Tomita-san overdid it... he peeled the skin off his hands, look... poor thing...
Their battered hands were painful to look at, but their fighting spirit came through.
I get you guys. You wanted to shine on the big stage...!!
ZBAAAAAAAAAAANG!!
I could hear the ferocious sound of that so-called Kameda-kun, the Okinomiya Titans' ringer, doing pitching practice.
He really is... no joke.
No wonder they call him beyond high-school level.
...But that level is obviously not something that should be barging into a sandlot game, grudge match or not. He has to understand that difference in class himself...
...That bastard. He threw seriously even against these guys...?
...Yeah. ...Rena thinks this is childish.
Rena's eyes hardened.
...For Rena to get angry, this has to be bad.
Tomita-kun and Okamura-kun were both sniffling, gritting their teeth and holding back tears.
...That savage smack of mitt and ball rang out again.
...When I turned around... that lefty, Kameda-kun or whatever, was looking right at us with a fearless grin...!
So that's how it is. ...Now that it's come to this, we've got no choice but to do it. Full battle mode!
Yeah! Looks like... we've got to go all out!!
And, yeah... we did try going all out. Then Mii-chan had a spectacular self-destruct...
Mion scratched her head bashfully, then a beat later folded over with an owowow...
Self-destruct...? What in the world did you do to self-destruct this badly...?
It would take absurdly long to explain... The one thing I can say is that after all that, we had nothing to show for it.
...Uuugh... Even after putting my body on the line this much... we couldn't score a single run...
...Our hard-won secret plan was ruined. Poor thing, poor thing.
I can't even imagine what kind of secret plan it was, but at any rate, it must've been outrageous enough to earn injuries like that.
...But hey. Thanks to it failing, at least this is still baseball, right?
Ahahahahaha... Yeah. ...I think Mii-chan's trump card was maybe a little too un-baseball-like. ...Maybe.
If it's come to this, then we have no choice, do we?
That means we have no choice but to settle this fair and square, the baseball way!!
Right, right! We have to settle this like baseball players! Ahahahahahahaha!
...A normal person would,
think this is where you give up and take the straightforward approach, but
as a member of our club, I wasn't about to swallow that at face value.

...It's that like baseball part that catches in my mind.
...As long as it looks like baseball, any means are fine...
that's what you mean... right...?

Ahahahahaha.
Mm-hmm, that's our club's creed, after all☆

Exactly so!
Not choosing our means is the very virtue of our club, you know.
Ohohohoho!
Rena and Satoko share a crisp, merry laugh.
...It's not exactly a virtue to brag about, but the best thing about our club is that there's not a trace of self-consciousness in it.
...But with cameras lined up all over, it's hard to pull any dirty tricks.
Rika-chan spread both arms and indicated the reporters lined up in the outfield.
...True enough,
with that many cameramen aiming huge telephoto lenses and watching us so closely, dirty play is hard to pull off in all sorts of ways.

Right, right.
...That was the bottleneck.
...With this many eyes on us, it's hard to make a really bold move.
...Ow ow ow ow...
...I never thought Mii-chan's methods were exactly ladylike, though.
Both Satoko and Rika-chan nodded along behind Rena.

Then let's get back to the main point.
...Right now, our team is losing by one run.

And it's already the bottom of the ninth...
and we've already got two outs against us.

...If this old man's strategy had worked...
we should've had the bases loaded by now...!

There's no point lamenting a failed strategy!
It's by not giving in and making the next move that our club proves itself!!
As if to cheer up Mion, dejected from fumbling her secret plan, Satoko puffed herself up with spirit!
Mion's secret plan failed, but Satoko looked as though she still had another move in reserve...
Satoko's got some real fire in her, huh?... Which means... she's got some hidden card up her sleeve...?
Keiichi-kun. Who do you think this girl is? It's Satoko-chan, you know? Houjou Satoko-chan!
...Right.
Isn't "Trapmaster Satoko" her very nickname?!
At the very, very end, she catches her opponent at the perfect moment.
Anyone in our club knows this: against Satoko, you must never let your guard down until the very last second!
...I may have trained her myself, but she's grown in terrifying ways. When it comes to taking a razor-thin lead, even I have to tip my hat sometimes.
...By the way, the next batter is Mii, and the batter after that is Satoko.
We're down by one, and if the next batter can get on base...
...then the following batter could still end it with one hit...
And that last batter is Satoko...?!
Ufufufufu...! See? Feels like we can win now, doesn't it?
...I can't even imagine what kind of setup it is, but I can say for sure it's some kind of trap. If I were the opposing pitcher, I'd intentionally walk her.
I can't even begin to imagine Satoko hitting a home run.
...But it's even harder to imagine her stepping into the batter's box honestly, with no plan at all!
If that Satoko called me out here, then... I see. I'm starting to read this...
...So Satoko's trap needs me as one of its pieces!
This is exciting! I wonder what Satoko-chan is going to do. I wonder!
...I'm looking forward to Satoko's walk-off home run too.
And for that, Kei-chan's role is indispensable.
...You understand, right?! This is our last chance for a comeback. We absolutely cannot fail!
Oh, yeah! I know that already!! Just watch! Satoko and I are gonna show you one massive comeback!!
Well then, Keiichi-san, I'll explain the procedure now. Lend me your ear for a moment.
Satoko and I crouched down behind cover and held a discussion that looked suspicious from the start.
...I wonder what Maebara-san and Houjou are talking about.
......I just pray they don't self-destruct with some outrageous trump card like the class president did...
Tomita-kun, Okamura-kun, and the rest of our teammates looked on with obvious anxiety.
Rena flashed them a beaming smile, as if to blow all their worries away.
It'll be okay, everyone. ...If it's Satoko-chan and Keiichi-kun, they'll definitely pull it off!
But Ryuugu-san, we're up against a Koshien-level pitcher...?! No matter how good Houjou's traps are...
Kuh-huh-huh... Well now, everyone, just sit back and watch. ...You're about to see one hell of a game. Kuh-huh-huh-huh!
Mion, who knew everything that was about to unfold, seemed to be struggling to suppress the laughter welling up inside her.
...As an enemy, she was utterly terrifying, but as an ally, utterly reassuring!
Mion waved to the umpire.
We have a pinch hitter! Substituting in for me, Mion Sonozaki -- Keiichi Maebara!
...Are you really sure about him?
The umpire cast an uneasy glance at the pinch hitter who had charged in wielding a golf club.
Ufufufufu! You'd better not underestimate Keiichi-kun~
The club members alone wore grins of absolute confidence, smirking knowingly.
